title: Quiz: Learning-Based Error Mitigation
description: Test your understanding of machine learning approaches to error mitigation

questions.question: What is the key idea behind Clifford Data Regression (CDR)?

options.text: Use classically simulable Clifford circuits to learn the noise-to-ideal mapping

questions.explanation: CDR uses Clifford circuits because they can be efficiently simulated classically, providing exact ideal values to learn the noise-to-ideal mapping.

questions.question: Why are Clifford circuits useful for training error mitigators?

options.text: They can be simulated classically in polynomial time
They always produce the same output

questions.explanation: Clifford circuits can be efficiently simulated classically using the Gottesman-Knill theorem, providing exact ideal outputs for training.

questions.question: What type of model is commonly used in neural network mitigators?
questions.explanation: MLPs with nonlinear activations can learn complex noise-to-ideal mappings and are commonly used for neural network mitigators.

questions.question: Transfer learning for mitigation can reuse a mitigator trained on one circuit for similar circuits.
questions.explanation: True. Transfer learning allows a mitigator trained on one circuit to be fine-tuned for similar circuits, reducing training overhead.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of learning-based mitigation over analytical methods?

options.text: Can adapt to unknown or complex noise without explicit noise models

questions.explanation: Learning-based methods can adapt to complex, unknown noise patterns without requiring explicit noise characterization.

questions.question: How can physics-based methods be combined with machine learning for mitigation?

options.text: Use ZNE noise-scaled values as features for a neural network
Only use one method at a time

questions.explanation: Hybrid methods use physics-based features (like ZNE noise-scaled data) as inputs to neural networks, combining physical insight with learning.

questions.question: What is the purpose of cross-validation in training mitigators?

options.text: To estimate generalization error and prevent overfitting
To reduce the number of training circuits

questions.explanation: Cross-validation helps estimate how well the mitigator generalizes to unseen circuits and prevents overfitting to training data.

questions.question: Which are challenges for learning-based error mitigation? (Select all)

options.text: Need for training data from Clifford or near-Clifford circuits
Generalization to circuits different from training set
Always requires fewer shots than other methods

questions.explanation: Learning-based methods require training data, may not generalize well to very different circuits, and have computational training costs. They don't necessarily require fewer shots.
